item.title: Hurricanes and Chiefs to contest Super Rugby Pacific grand final
item.description: Hurricanes and Chiefs to contest Super Rugby Pacific grand final

item.title: FRU stands by decision to part ways with Mick Byrne
item.description: FRU stands by decision to part ways with Mick Byrne

item.title: Gospel High and Suva Grammar record dominant wins in Southern Zone Rugby Competition
item.description: Gospel High School and Suva Grammar School delivered some of the most dominant performances of the day as Round 2 of the Southern Zone Schools Rugby Competition produced a number of one-sided victories and high-scoring encounters. Matches are being played at the Sports Council Facility, Albert Park Ground and the Suva Grammar School Grounds for the 2026 Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Competition, wh
item.content:encoded:
Gospel High School and Suva Grammar School delivered some of the most dominant performances of the day as Round 2 of the Southern Zone Schools Rugby Competition produced a number of one-sided victories and high-scoring encounters.

Matches are being played at the Sports Council Facility, Albert Park Ground and the Suva Grammar School Grounds for the 2026 Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Competition, where six schools are battling for a place in the next round.

Suva Grammar School recorded convincing wins in both the Under-16 and Under-17 grades at their home ground.

The Grammar Under-16 side overwhelmed Marist Champagnat Institute 69-5, while their Under-17 team comfortably defeated Jai Narayan College 38-0.

Gospel High School also made a strong statement in the competition, thrashing Ratu Sukuna Memorial School 67-0 in the Under-15 grade.

The school's Under-16 side backed up that performance with a hard-fought 24-20 victory over Lomaivuna High School.

In the Under-14 grade, Suva Grammar School beat Nasinu Secondary School 19-10, while Church College Fiji was awarded a win by default against Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School.

At Suva Grammar Ground 2, Dudley High School secured a 37-24 win over Mahatma Gandhi Memorial School in the Under-16 grade.

Lomaivuna High School responded with a 17-10 victory over Dudley High School in the Raluve Under-16 competition.

Naitasiri Secondary School also produced an impressive attacking display to defeat John Wesley College 48-26 in the Under-17 grade.

With several pool matches still to be completed, supporters can expect more exciting rugby as teams continue their push for a place in the next stage of the 2026 Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Competition.

item.title: QVS edges RKS in thrilling U18 Eastern Zone clash 15 - 13
item.description: Queen Victoria School successfully defended their home ground after edging Ratu Kadavulevu School 15-13 in a closely contested Und

item.title: Lelean outclass St John's 41-9 in the Dean's Eastern Zone clash
item.description: Lelean Memorial School U18 team delivered a commanding second-half performance to defeat St John's College Cawaci 41-9 in their Vodafone Dean's Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union Competition match at Nukuvuto. Lelean led 17-3 at halftime after executing their game plan effectively despite challenging wet weather conditions.
item.content:encoded:
Lelean Memorial School U18 team delivered a commanding second-half performance to defeat St John's College Cawaci 41-9 in their Vodafone Dean's Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union Competition match at Nukuvuto.

Lelean led 17-3 at halftime after executing their game plan effectively despite challenging wet weather conditions.

Speaking to fijivillage News, head coach Meli Serukalou says the team remained focused on sticking to its structures against a strong St John's side.

He noted that the wet conditions had influenced their approach, with greater emphasis placed on defence, set pieces and maintaining possession.

Serukalou also stressed the importance of discipline in the second half, and his side responded by extending their lead with a dominant display after the interval.

Meanwhile, Ratu Kadavulevu School's Under-14 side thrashed Tailevu North College 50-5, while the RKS Under-15 team defeated Saint Vincent College 39-7.

In another closely contested match, Queen Victoria School edged RKS 8-7.

item.title: USA cruise past Paraguay 4-1 in dominant World Cup opener
item.description: Co-hosts USA made an impressive start to their FIFA World Cup campaign with a commanding 4-1 victory over Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium. The Americans scored three goals in the first half and maintained control throughout the match to secure all three points.
item.content:encoded:
Co-hosts USA made an impressive start to their FIFA World Cup campaign with a commanding 4-1 victory over Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium.

The Americans scored three goals in the first half and maintained control throughout the match to secure all three points.

Damián Bobadilla, Folarin Balogun and Giovanni Reyna found the back of the net for the USA.

The result puts the USA in a strong position in Group D as they begin their quest for World Cup success on home soil.

Four more World Cup matches will be played tomorrow, with much of the attention expected to be on the highly anticipated clash between Brazil and Morocco.

Meanwhile, Canada fought back to earn a 1-1 draw against Bosnia and Herzegovina in the FIFA World Cup match this morning at Toronto Stadium.

item.title: South Korea come from behind to beat Czech Republic 2-1
item.description: Substitute Oh Hyeon-Gyu emerged as South Korea's hero, scoring a late winner as they came from behind to beat the Czech Republic 2
